codeExamples.title: Inclusion-Exclusion
codeExamples.explanation: Applies inclusion-exclusion to count elements in union of sets

codeExamples.title: Pigeonhole Check
codeExamples.code:
def pigeonhole_min(items, containers):
    """Minimum items in most-filled container"""
    import math
    return math.ceil(items / containers)

# Example: 25 students, 6 groups
min_per_group = pigeonhole_min(25, 6)
print(f'At least one group has >= {min_per_group} students')
# Output: 5
codeExamples.explanation: Calculates minimum using generalized pigeonhole principle
